200.description:
**When `enable_streaming` is `true`:** Server-Sent Events stream.

**When `enable_streaming` is `false`:** JSON response (see schema below).

| Event | When |
|---|---|
| `thread_id` | Always the first SSE event. Carries `thread_id` (the thread created or reused for this invocation). |
Each event follows the SSE wire format:

```
event: <event_name>
data: <json_payload>


```

**Core event types**

| Event | When |
|---|---|
| `message_start` | Always first (after `thread_id` on the agents endpoint). Carries `message.id`, `message.role`, `message.model`. |
| `content_block_start` | Opens a block. `content_block.type` is `text`, `tool_use`, or `guardrail`. |
| `content_block_delta` | Text chunk within a `text` block (`delta.type = "text_delta"`). |
| `content_block_stop` | Closes the current block. |
| `tool_result` | Tool output after a `tool_use` block (only when `show_tool_calls` is enabled). Carries `tool_use_id`, `tool_type`, `content`, `is_error`, `error_details`, `metadata`. |
| `message_id` | DB id of the persisted message (only when `thread_id` is set). |
| `usage` | Cumulative token counts: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cached_tokens`. |
| `error` | Stream error. `error.type` is one of `context_length_exceeded`, `llm_error`, `http_error`, `internal_error`. |

**Guardrail blocks** (`content_block.type = "guardrail"`) carry the replacement text in `content_block.content` and have no `content_block_delta` events.

See `SSE_EVENTS_DOCUMENTATION.md` for full payload schemas and flow examples.

On error, `error` is the terminal event — **no** `message_stop` follows.

get.description:
List tracked executions with optional filtering and pagination.

Query Parameters:
- date_from: Start date (ISO format, default: 7 days ago)
- date_to: End date (ISO format, default: today)
- execution_type: Filter by type (AGENT_RUN, EXPERIMENT_ROW, BATCH_JOB, etc.)
- status: Filter by status (COMPLETED, FAILED, PENDING, etc.)
- agent_id: Filter by agent
- thread_id: Filter by thread
- experiment_id: Filter by experiment
- batch_id: Filter by batch
- workflow_id: Filter by workflow
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- page_size: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

Returns:
- List of executions (without usage_records for performance)
- Pagination metadata

Note: To get usage records for a specific execution, call GET /usage/executions/{id}

post.description: Register a high-level tracked execution.

schema.title: Response Create Execution Usage Executions Post

post.description: Create a thread for an agent

get.description:
List threads for a specific agent with pagination.

Query Parameters:
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- page_size: Items per page (default: 20)
- order: Sort order ('asc' or 'desc', default: 'desc')
- include_messages: Whether to include messages (default: True)

Returns:
- Paginated list of threads with messages for the specified agent
Get thread info and messages

Get a sub-thread produced by an agent_tool invocation, with its messages.

Authorization is transitive via the invocation lineage rather than by project
membership: the caller's project must appear somewhere in the sub-thread's
execution chain. This lets the project that started an agent_tool chain render
the resulting conversation even when the invoked agent is a core_agent that
lives in another project (its sub-thread's own execution carries that other
project, but the chain traces back to the originating one). Works at any
nesting depth (agent inside agent).

Return threads with an active (PENDING/RUNNING) agent execution.

Unlike `GET /threads`, this counts threads even when they have no title or
messages yet, so the UI can show "N chats running" while their agents are
still producing the first response.

List threads with pagination.

Query Parameters:
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- page_size: Items per page (default: 20)
- order: Sort order ('asc' or 'desc', default: 'desc')
- userId: Filter by user ID
- externalRef: Filter by external reference
- agentId: Filter by agent ID
- date_from: Filter by created date (from, format: YYYY-MM-DD)
- date_to: Filter by created date (to, format: YYYY-MM-DD)
- include_messages: Whether to include messages (default: True)

Returns:
- Paginated list of threads with messages

post.description:
Register one usage report from a microservice (high-level).

Requires **Users-Management-Key** header. Send a single payload with context
(project, type, status, agent, thread, etc.) and zero or more usage items
(LLM calls, scraping, rerank, etc.). The API creates the underlying
execution and usage records.

Returns ``id`` (use with GET /usage/executions/{id} for details) and
``usage_count`` (number of usage items recorded).

schema.title: Response Register Usage Report Usage Report Post

post.description:
Register a usage record from a microservice.

Requires **Users-Management-Key** header. Use this when an external service
(e.g. worker, agent runtime) needs to record usage (LLM tokens, scraping,
rerank, etc.). Optionally link to a tracked execution via execution_id.

schema.title: Response Register Usage Record Usage Records Post
Response List Project Requests Usage Requests Get
Response Get Threads Usage Usage Threads Get
Response Get Llm Models Usage Usage Llm Get
Response Get Office Documents Usage Usage Office Documents Get
Response Get Document Validations Usage Usage Document Validations Get
Response Get Document Conversions Usage Usage Document Conversions Get
Response Get Rerank Usage Usage Rerank Get
Response Get Embeddings Usage Usage Embeddings Get
Response Get Scraping Usage Usage Scraping Get
Response Get Web Search Usage Usage Web Search Get

200.description:
Server-Sent Events stream.

Each event follows the SSE wire format:

```
event: <event_name>
data: <json_payload>


```

**Core event types**

| Event | When |
|---|---|
| `message_start` | Always first (after `thread_id` on the agents endpoint). Carries `message.id`, `message.role`, `message.model`. |
| `content_block_start` | Opens a block. `content_block.type` is `text`, `tool_use`, or `guardrail`. |
| `content_block_delta` | Text chunk within a `text` block (`delta.type = "text_delta"`). |
| `content_block_stop` | Closes the current block. |
| `tool_result` | Tool output after a `tool_use` block (only when `show_tool_calls` is enabled). Carries `tool_use_id`, `tool_type`, `content`, `is_error`, `error_details`, `metadata`. |
| `message_id` | DB id of the persisted message (only when `thread_id` is set). |
| `usage` | Cumulative token counts: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cached_tokens`. |
| `error` | Stream error. `error.type` is one of `context_length_exceeded`, `llm_error`, `http_error`, `internal_error`. |

**Guardrail blocks** (`content_block.type = "guardrail"`) carry the replacement text in `content_block.content` and have no `content_block_delta` events.

See `SSE_EVENTS_DOCUMENTATION.md` for full payload schemas and flow examples.

`message_stop` is always the last event — on success **and** after `error`.

get.description: List executions for a workflow.
Get a curated workflow execution detail with highlighted outputs and agent threads.

Lightweight status snapshot of a workflow execution.

Returns per-step status and aggregated usage totals (input/output tokens).
Does NOT return step `input`/`output` payloads or full usage records — use
GET .../steps/{step_id} for the typed result of a specific step.

Full payload of a single workflow step execution.

The `node_type` field indicates how to interpret `output` (which varies per
node type — e.g. firecrawl returns scrape data, http returns
{status_code, headers, body}, plai_agent returns thread+response, etc.).

post.description:
Force-cancel a workflow execution, even if it appears stuck in RUNNING.

Useful when an execution is wedged after a transient infra failure
(e.g. a dropped DB connection mid-tick) and needs to be unblocked so the
user can relaunch it.
Endpoint for receiving Pub/Sub messages for workflows.

schema.title: Response Workflows Pubsub Workflows Pubsub Post

get.description:
Get the complete RBAC configuration for project-level permissions.

This endpoint returns all available roles, their permissions, and descriptions
to help the UI team build role-aware interfaces.

**Authentication:** None required (public endpoint).

**Returns:**
- `roles`: List of all roles with their permissions and descriptions
- `all_permissions`: List of all available project-level permissions

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "roles": [
    {
      "role": "OWNER",
      "permissions": ["view_project", "update_project", "delete_project", ...],
      "description": "Full access to all features..."
    },
    ...
  ],
  "all_permissions": ["view_project", "update_project", ...]
}
```

Get the complete RBAC configuration for organization-level permissions.

This endpoint returns all available roles, their permissions, and descriptions
to help the UI team build role-aware interfaces.

**Authentication:** None required (public endpoint).

**Returns:**
- `roles`: List of all roles with their permissions and descriptions
- `all_permissions`: List of all available organization-level permissions

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "roles": [
    {
      "role": "OWNER",
      "permissions": ["view_organization", "update_organization", ...],
      "description": "Full access to all features..."
    },
    ...
  ],
  "all_permissions": ["view_organization", "update_organization", ...]
}
```

post.description:
Check if a specific role has a specific project permission.

This endpoint allows the UI team to programmatically check permissions
without parsing the entire RBAC configuration.

**Authentication:** None required (public endpoint).

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "role": "MEMBER",
  "permission": "delete_agent"
}
```

**Returns:**
```json
{
  "has_permission": false,
  "role": "MEMBER",
  "permission": "delete_agent"
}
```

Check if a specific role has a specific organization permission.

This endpoint allows the UI team to programmatically check permissions
without parsing the entire RBAC configuration.

**Authentication:** None required (public endpoint).

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "role": "MEMBER",
  "permission": "create_project"
}
```

**Returns:**
```json
{
  "has_permission": true,
  "role": "MEMBER",
  "permission": "create_project"
}
```

Crea una nueva credencial en la organización.

Tipos soportados:
- SIMPLE: API keys simples (key/value)
- GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT: Google Service Account JSON
- HEADER: Headers HTTP personalizados
- BEARER: Bearer token authentication
- MCP: Model Context Protocol servers
- OAUTH: OAuth access/refresh tokens

Requiere permisos de organización para crear credenciales.
Los project_ids se especifican en el payload.

get.description:
Lista todas las credenciales de la organización.

Filtros opcionales:
- type: Tipo de credencial (SIMPLE, GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT, etc.)
Requiere permisos de organización para ver credenciales.

Obtiene una credencial por ID.

Nota: Los campos sensibles se retornan enmascarados (***ENCRYPTED***)
Requiere permisos de organización para ver credenciales.

patch.description:
Actualiza una credencial existente.

Solo se pueden actualizar:
- name: Nombre de la credencial
- config: Configuración (debe ser válida para el tipo)

No se puede cambiar el tipo de credencial.
Requiere permisos de organización para actualizar credenciales.

delete.description:
Elimina una credencial.

Advertencia: Si la credencial está siendo usada por tools,
esas tools dejarán de funcionar.
Requiere permisos de organización para eliminar credenciales.

post.description:
Endpoint llamado por Cloud Scheduler cada minuto para ejecutar jobs pendientes

Authentication: Management Key (no requiere token de proyecto)

Este endpoint:
1. Verifica la management key
2. Adquiere advisory lock de Postgres
3. Encuentra jobs que deben ejecutarse (next_run_at <= now)
4. Para cada job:
   - Crea un JobExecution
   - Invoca el workflow correspondiente
   - Actualiza last_run_at y next_run_at

Returns:
    Resumen de jobs procesados

High-level body for POST /usage/report.

Client sends one "report" (context + usage items). The API creates the
underlying execution and usage records; the client does not need to know
about TrackedExecution or UsageRecord.

WorkflowDefinition-Output.title: WorkflowDefinition
WorkflowDefinition-Output.description: The full graph structure of a workflow.

WorkflowStepAncestor.title: WorkflowStepAncestor
WorkflowStepAncestor.description:
A container that wraps a workflow step in the execution tree.

Ancestors are returned root-first so consumers can render nesting context
(e.g. a subworkflow badge, a loop iteration chip) without re-parsing
`node_id`. Only containers that contribute a segment to the runtime
`node_id` are emitted — today that means `SubworkflowNode` and
`LoopNode`. `parallel` and `sequential` containers don't prefix the
runtime id, so they're omitted.

WorkflowStepExecutionStatusItem.title: WorkflowStepExecutionStatusItem
WorkflowStepExecutionStatusItem.description:
Lightweight per-step summary used inside the /status endpoint.

Excludes input/output payloads and usage records so the response stays
cheap to poll. Fetch full payloads via GET .../steps/{step_id}.

`total_input_tokens`/`total_output_tokens` are only present for step
types that actually emit `UsageRecord`s (today: `plai_agent`). For
`firecrawl`, `http`, `markdown_report`, etc. they stay `None`.

WorkflowStepResultResponse.title: WorkflowStepResultResponse
WorkflowStepResultResponse.description:
Full result of a single workflow step, including input, output and usage.

`node_type` matches `WorkflowNodeType` and tells the consumer how to
interpret `output` (whose shape varies per node type).
